commands.author: recallmenot

target.title: Signal: note-to-self
target.body:
## Summary

### What problem does this PR solve?

- Adds explicit Signal `ingressMode: "note-to-self"` support so linked-device `signal-cli` setups can use the operator's Signal Note to Self chat as the OpenClaw conversation surface.
- Removes the practical need to buy, register, and maintain a second phone number and Signal account just to run OpenClaw as a personal Signal bot.
- Promotes only self-addressed `syncMessage.sentMessage` envelopes into the normal inbound pipeline, while continuing to drop sent transcripts addressed to other people and direct own-account non-sync echoes.
- Adds self-reply echo suppression so OpenClaw replies sent into Note to Self do not re-enter as fresh prompts.

### Why does this matter now?

- Today, a reliable Signal setup effectively assumes a dedicated bot number/account. That is a high-friction requirement for the common personal use case where the operator only wants to message their own OpenClaw instance through Signal, not expose OpenClaw to contacts.
- Signal Note to Self is the natural secure chat surface for that use case, but linked-device Note to Self messages arrive as sent-sync events that the existing loop protection drops before agent dispatch.
- The prior PR for this issue proved the core carve-out but lacked explicit opt-in, tests, UUID handling, attachments/edits coverage, approval reaction coverage, and stronger self-reply loop protection.

### What is the intended outcome?

- Existing `standard` Signal deployments keep their current behavior.
- Users who opt into `channels.signal.ingressMode="note-to-self"` can send text, attachments, edits, and approval reactions through their own Signal Note to Self chat.
- OpenClaw ignores sent-sync transcripts to other Signal recipients and avoids looping on its own Note to Self replies.
- After community testing and follow-up hardening, this mode could become the default-recommended Signal gateway setup for personal deployments in a future PR.

### What is intentionally out of scope?

- No global/core `dmPolicy` changes.
- No change to the default-recommended Signal setup in this PR.
- No required `accountUuid` config for normal users; UUID discovery reads the local signal-cli account store instead.
- No durable persistence for Note to Self self-reply echo markers across gateway restarts; replay suppression is intentionally process-local.
- No live test against a third-party Signal contact, to avoid contacting or spamming real people.

### What does success look like?

- Focused Signal tests pass for account discovery, Note to Self sync promotion, UUID-only routing, edits, attachment-only messages, approval reactions, echo suppression, and legacy approval-reaction key compatibility.
- A real linked-device Signal setup can process phone-originated Note to Self text, image-only, edited-message, and approval-reaction flows without observed self-reply loops.

### What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether `ingressMode="note-to-self"` is bounded to Signal and remains opt-in.
- Whether self-addressed sent-sync promotion cannot accidentally process transcripts to other recipients.
- Whether echo suppression is strong enough without adding persistent state.
- Whether UUID normalization/discovery and approval reaction matching preserve existing behavior.

## Linked context

### Which issue does this close?

Closes #75870.

### Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Supersedes and extends #75890.

Related #5722.

### Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No direct maintainer request cited; this follows the linked issue and earlier PR context.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Linked-device Signal deployments can route user-authored Note to Self messages into OpenClaw without processing sent transcripts to other contacts or looping on OpenClaw's own replies.
- Real environment tested: Local focused Vitest coverage and branch autoreview in the OpenClaw checkout. Also verified against a real local linked-device Signal setup using `signal-cli 0.14.3`, an installed local OpenClaw build from this branch, and `channels.signal.ingressMode="note-to-self"` with `receiveMode="on-start"`, `dmPolicy="disabled"`, and `groupPolicy="disabled"`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test extensions/signal/src/account-store.test.ts extensions/signal/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-auth.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor.tool-result.autostart.test.ts extensions/signal/src/normalize.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts extensions/signal/src/self-reply-echoes.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm config:channels:check`; `pnpm lint --threads=8`; `pnpm tsgo:prod`; `pnpm check:test-types`; `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`; `git diff --check`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`; `pnpm build`; `npm pack`; install the packed local build; `openclaw gateway restart`; `openclaw status --deep`; `openclaw logs --limit 80 --plain`; phone-originated Signal Note to Self text prompt requesting a unique reply token; phone-originated attachment-only Note to Self image prompt; edited Note to Self prompt changing the requested reply token; `/diagnostics note-to-self approval reaction test` approved by reacting `👍` in Signal; post-CI-fix local build installed and retested with a Note to Self prompt; another Signal account sent a normal direct message to the configured Signal number; the configured Signal account sent a normal direct message to another Signal account.
- Evidence after fix: Focused tests cover Note to Self sent-sync promotion, third-party sent transcript drops, UUID-only paths, edits, attachment-only messages, approval reactions, account UUID discovery, outbound echo recording, timestamp-less fallback suppression, and legacy approval-reaction key compatibility. Live proof showed Signal channel startup and reply delivery in the gateway log:

```text
channels/signal [default] starting provider (http://127.0.0.1:8080)
signal-cli: INFO HttpServerHandler - Started HTTP server on /127.0.0.1:8080
channels/signal delivered reply to <redacted-own-signal-account>
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused Signal suite passed with 138 tests, channel config, lint, production type, test type, package-boundary compile, and whitespace checks passed, autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings, and deep status reported `Signal ON OK configured`. A phone-originated text Note to Self prompt produced the requested unique token, an attachment-only image prompt reached a vision-capable model and the reply described the image text, and editing the original Signal message produced a second reply with the edited requested token; none of those paths produced an observed self-reply echo loop. After the CI-fix commit, an installed local build replied to another phone-originated Note to Self prompt with the requested unique token. A normal direct message from another Signal account arrived on the configured Signal account's phone and linked signal-cli instance, but OpenClaw did not reply, did not send a pairing request, and did not appear to start an agent turn for that sender. A normal direct message sent from the configured Signal account to another Signal account appeared in signal-cli and on both phones as a regular Signal message, but OpenClaw did not reply or otherwise act on that outbound sent-sync transcript. The Signal approval reaction also resolved the diagnostic exec approval once: `Exec approval allowed once. Resolved by Signal approval (<redacted-own-signal-account>).`
- What was not tested: No remaining known live-proof gap for the Note to Self text/image/edit/approval, inbound third-party DM ignore, or outbound third-party sent-sync ignore paths. Broader device/version coverage is left to CI and maintainer/community testing.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: A daemon-originated self-send was tested and confirmed not to emit a receive event on `/api/v1/events`; the successful live proof used the primary phone app, which is the real user path for Note to Self.
- Before evidence: Issue #75870 and PR #75890 document the prior behavior: linked-device Note to Self messages arrived as sent-sync events and were dropped by loop protection before agent dispatch.

## Tests and validation

### Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm test extensions/signal/src/account-store.test.ts extensions/signal/src/accounts.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-auth.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/signal/src/approval-reactions.test.ts extensions/signal/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor/event-handler.inbound-context.test.ts extensions/signal/src/monitor.tool-result.autostart.test.ts extensions/signal/src/normalize.test.ts extensions/signal/src/send.test.ts extensions/signal/src/self-reply-echoes.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`

### What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Account UUID discovery from signal-cli config/account-store layouts.
- Parse-compatible `accountUuid` handling for shipped string configs, with runtime UUID normalization for bare, compact, and `uuid:<id>` forms.
- Note to Self sync-message promotion for text, nested messages, edits, and attachment-only messages.
- Third-party sent transcript drops for phone and UUID destination mismatches.
- Self-reply echo suppression for timestamp IDs, timestamp-less text/media fallbacks, provisional pre-send guards, and late UUID discovery.
- Signal approval reaction matching across phone, compact UUID, hyphenated UUID, and legacy persisted reaction keys.
- Default/named account inheritance behavior for Note to Self mode and `accountUuid`.

### What failed before this fix, if known?

- Linked-device Note to Self prompts arrived as `syncMessage.sentMessage` and were treated as outbound/own-account loop candidates instead of inbound user prompts.
- The earlier one-off carve-out did not cover the full tested surface for UUID identity, attachments, edits, approval reactions, and self-reply echoes.

### If no test was added, why not?

- Not applicable; focused regression coverage was added and live behavior was verified.

## Risk checklist

### Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Signal users can opt into a new `note-to-self` ingress mode.

### Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This adds `channels.signal.ingressMode` and documents optional `channels.signal.accountUuid`, plus generated channel config metadata and docs. Existing configs keep the default `standard` mode; `accountUuid` remains parse-compatible with shipped string configs and only valid UUID forms are used for runtime identity matching.

### Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, bounded to Signal channel ingress and native approval reaction handling. There are no new external network calls or provider/tool permissions.

### What is the highest-risk area?

- Accidentally treating sent transcripts to other Signal recipients as user prompts, or letting OpenClaw's own Note to Self replies loop back into the agent.

### How is that risk mitigated?

- The mode is explicit opt-in and Signal-local.
- Self-addressed sent-sync promotion checks source/destination identity, including UUID-only envelopes.
- Sent transcripts to other recipients are dropped before dispatch.
- Self-reply echo suppression records outbound replies and timestamp-less text/media fallbacks in process memory.
- Focused tests cover destination mismatch drops, UUID normalization, edits, attachments, approval reactions, and echo suppression.

## Current review state

### What is the next action?

- Ready for maintainer review.

### What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Nothing known from the author side. CI and maintainer review are still needed after PR creation.

### Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Local branch autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings after the initial review fixes. ClawSweeper's follow-up `accountUuid` compatibility finding was addressed by restoring permissive schema parsing and moving UUID validation to runtime normalization.

## Notes For Maintainers

- This intentionally does not edit `CHANGELOG.md`; release-owned changelog generation should pick up the PR/commit context.
- The schema intentionally accepts bare compact or hyphenated UUIDs only. It does not accept `uuid:`-prefixed config values because that would expand the public config contract; normal users should rely on auto-discovery instead.
- If a maintainer wants live Signal proof before merge, the code is structured so the requested scenario can be demonstrated without changing the PR: configure `ingressMode: "note-to-self"`, start the Signal monitor, and send a Note to Self message from the primary device.
target.author: recallmenot

commands.reason: matching ClawSweeper response comment already exists
